description:
You are the release manager who must verify that upcoming application releases fit within the disk-space budget of the deployment servers.

    Current project layout (already present in the system):
      /home/user/releases/
          ├── app_v1.0/
          │     ├── README.txt
          │     └── bin/app.bin
          ├── app_v1.1/
          │     ├── README.txt
          │     ├── bin/app.bin
          │     └── docs/CHANGELOG.md
          └── app_v2.0/
                ├── README.txt
                ├── bin/app.bin
                └── scripts/migrate.sh

    Task goals (all steps are mandatory):

    1. Create the directory /home/user/release_audit if it does not yet exist.  
       • The directory must end up with default user-writable permissions (e.g. 755 or 700).

    2. Compute the *exact* aggregate size, in bytes, of every **first-level** sub-directory located in /home/user/releases.  
       • “Exact” means the sum of the byte-sizes of all regular files inside a release (do **not** include the size of the directories themselves).  
       • Ignore hidden files (none exist now, but the rule must be followed).

    3. Write those numbers to a comma-separated values (CSV) log located at:  
          /home/user/release_audit/disk_usage_report.csv

       The CSV **must contain exactly five lines**, formatted precisely like this (including capitalisation, commas and line breaks <LF>, **no extra spaces**):

          Release_Name,Size_Bytes
          app_v1.0,<bytes_for_app_v1.0>
          app_v1.1,<bytes_for_app_v1.1>
          app_v2.0,<bytes_for_app_v2.0>
          ALL_RELEASES,<total_bytes_all_releases>

       • Replace the <bytes_for…> placeholders with the computed integer byte counts.  
       • The release rows must appear in strict ASCII alphabetical order.  
       • The final ALL_RELEASES line must equal the sum of the three individual sizes.  
       • Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline character.

    4. After creating disk_usage_report.csv, display its contents to the terminal so that a reviewer can immediately see the results.

    5. Do **not** hard-code the numbers—derive them with commands such as “find”, “stat”, “wc -c”, etc., then programmatically assemble the CSV file.

    Verification expectations:
       • The CSV file exists at /home/user/release_audit/disk_usage_report.csv.  
       • Its permission bits are user-writable.  
       • Its contents exactly match the required five-line format and the precalculated byte totals.  
       • The terminal output printed by the agent displays those same five lines.

    Hint: A portable approach is to pipe “find … ‑type f ‑printf '%s\n'” into “awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'”.
truth:
Initial state prepared by the test harness
    ------------------------------------------
    1. Paths & files that already exist before the agent starts:

       /home/user/releases/app_v1.0/README.txt            –  512 bytes
       /home/user/releases/app_v1.0/bin/app.bin           – 1536 bytes
                                                           ----------------
                                                           2048 bytes total for app_v1.0

       /home/user/releases/app_v1.1/README.txt            – 1024 bytes
       /home/user/releases/app_v1.1/bin/app.bin           – 2048 bytes
       /home/user/releases/app_v1.1/docs/CHANGELOG.md     – 3072 bytes
                                                           ----------------
                                                           6144 bytes total for app_v1.1

       /home/user/releases/app_v2.0/README.txt            – 2048 bytes
       /home/user/releases/app_v2.0/bin/app.bin           – 3072 bytes
       /home/user/releases/app_v2.0/scripts/migrate.sh    – 2048 bytes
                                                           ----------------
                                                           7168 bytes total for app_v2.0

       Grand total across every release directory: 15 360 bytes.

    2. Directory /home/user/release_audit does NOT exist at start-up.

    Expected end-state after the agent completes
    --------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/release_audit/            – directory created, normal user permissions.
    • /home/user/release_audit/disk_usage_report.csv – regular file, contents **exactly**:

          Release_Name,Size_Bytes
          app_v1.0,2048
          app_v1.1,6144
          app_v2.0,7168
          ALL_RELEASES,15360

      (Note: file must end with a single trailing newline.)

    The automated checker will:
       1. Ensure the file exists at the precise path.
       2. Validate that the file’s SHA-256 is the hash of the above five-line content including the newline.
       3. Confirm permissions allow the regular user to read the file.
       4. Scan the agent’s last command-output buffer for those same five lines.
